---
title: "Knowledge Management"
subtitle: "***Operationalizing the Regional Assessment***"
author: "*inSileco*"
date: "2026-04-14"
from: markdown+emoji
---

```{=html}
<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  var logo = document.querySelector('.slide-logo');
  if (logo) {
    var link = document.createElement('a');
    link.href = 'https://www.insileco.io';
    link.target = '_blank';
    logo.parentNode.insertBefore(link, logo);
    link.appendChild(logo);
  }
});
</script>
```

## Let us start at the end {.transition-slide .center}

### *What might the outputs of the assessment look like?*



## NCEA {#useful-output .full-image-slide data-background-image="img/ncea.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center center" data-background-color="white"}

::: {.notes}
Open with the end state rather than with methods. Use this as a visual anchor: this is the kind of regional output people can work from when the assessment is structured to be useful in practice.
:::

## What questions does this answer? {.transition-slide .center}

### *The current state of the region*

### *Risks to assessment priorities* {.fragment}

### *Pathways of effects* {.fragment}

### *Management & mitigation priorities* {.fragment}


## Then what? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Single set of answers*

### *Assessment quickly becomes outdated* {.fragment}

### *Accumulated knowledge is scattered* {.fragment}


## What if? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Single set of answers*

### ***Can be updated and reused***

### ***Value grows as knowledge accumulates***


## CEA Explorer {#cea_explorer .full-image-slide data-background-image="img/cea_explorer.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center center" data-background-color="white"}


## Where does this start from? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Interoperable metadata* 

<br>

::: {style="font-size: 120%;"}
::: {.takeaway .fragment}
***Which is simply properly planned and***

***executed knowledge management***
:::
:::

## Knowledge Management

::: {style="font-size: 90%;"}

:::{.callout-tip icon=false}
### Organize the knowledge

Clear terms, clear categories, clear sources.
:::

:::{.callout-important icon=false .fragment}
### Document it well

People can understand what it means and where it came from.
:::

:::{.callout-caution icon=false .fragment}
### Connect the information

Updates in one place can support many uses.
:::

:::{.callout-note icon=false .fragment}
### Build tools on top

Maps, summaries, and decision support tools become easier to maintain.
:::
:::


::: {.notes}
This is the light reverse-engineering slide. Avoid technical jargon. The message is simple: organized and connected knowledge makes updateable tools possible.
:::


## What does this look like? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Can be as simple as a shared google sheets* 


## ERSL Google sheet {#ersl_sheets .full-image-slide data-background-image="img/ersl_sheets.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center center" data-background-color="white"}


<!-- https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/111oHAae8iwm4S67ZW1SURDO-Zlp2gcTYpLiyhbMrdS8/edit?usp=sharing -->

## What do we do with it? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Knowledge Hubs* 

## ERSL Hub {#ersl_hub .full-image-slide data-background-image="img/ersl_hub.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center center" data-background-color="white"}

## What does this make possible? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Find & Connect* 

### *Update & Reuse* {.fragment}

### *Flexibility* {.fragment}

::: {.notes}
This is the missing bridge slide. The hub is not just a place to store information. It makes the knowledge easier to find, connect, update, and reuse across discussions and assessments.
:::

## Why does this matter? {.transition-slide .center}

### *Shared regional memory*

### *Updatable context* {.fragment}

### *Reuse across assessments* {.fragment}

### *Operational capacity* {.fragment}


## In a nutshell {.transition-slide .center}

***Levarages the Regional Assessment***

***to build capacity beyond the report***


## When the next project arrives {.transition-slide .center}

### *Build on existing context*

### *Assess risk (much!) faster*

### *Support defensible decisions*



## Project builder {#project_builder .full-image-slide data-background-image="img/project_builder.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center center" data-background-color="white"}


#

::: {style="font-size: 110%;"}

::: {.takeaway}
***A well-structured assessment is not just an answer***
:::

::: {.takeaway .fragment}
***It is an operational system for managing risk over time***
:::

::: {.takeaway .fragment}
***And it begins with proper knowledge management***
:::

:::

# 

::: {style="font-size: 120%;"}
***Thank you!***
:::
